reasoning: Analyzed the x86-64 disassembly section by section, identifying distinct function boundaries and basic blocks based on control-flow instructions (jmp, je, jne, jge, jl, call, ret).
Function at 0x1210 (cd command logic) contains basic blocks spanning 1210->1225, 1227->123f (fallthrough/jump), 1241->1251, 1253->125f, and a merge at 1261->126b.
Function at 0x1270 (help command logic) contains a loop structure with blocks at 1270->12aa, 12ad->12bf (loop header), 12c1->12e7 (loop body), and 12e9->1301 (loop exit).
Function at 0x1310 is a trivial stub returning 0.
Function at 0x1320 is a trivial stub returning 3.
Function at 0x1330 (process spawning with fork/execvp/waitpid) contains complex conditional jumps checking fork results (1344->1375) and a waitpid loop (138b->13ca).
Function at 0x13e0 (command execution router) loops through built-in commands using strcmp, mapping to blocks 13e8->13f4, 13ff->1418, 141a->1452, 1454->145f, and fallback 1461->1475.
Function at 0x1480 (line editor/reader loop) uses getchar inside a loop with buffer reallocation checks, branching at 14de, 14eb, and 1524.
Function at 0x1580 (string tokenizer) loops over strtok with buffer expansion checks, branching at 15ed, 1612, and 163f.
Function at 0x16b0 (main command loop) prints a prompt, reads input, tokenizes, executes, and loops back conditionally (16fe->16b8).
Function at 0x1710 is the entry point invoking the main loop at 16b0.
Basic blocks across all subroutines were mapped to sequential identifiers (B0 to B43) and edges were linked based on conditional/unconditional jumps and standard sequential execution flow.
